summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting that included a presentation from the Autism Society of Greater Cincinnati donating sensory kits to first responders, a public hearing on zoning case ZC-2021-002 requesting a zone change from R3 residential to M2 heavy manufacturing for a truck repair business at 6823 Goshen Road, and police department hiring actions. The zone change was denied by the trustees in accordance with Clermont County and the Goshen Zoning Commission's recommendations, with encouragement for the applicant to explore a variance. Three new police officers were appointed and Beggar's Night was scheduled for October 31.
decisions: Motion to amend agenda to add B1 (Beggar's Night scheduling) - approved
Motion to adopt agenda as amended - approved
Motion to approve payment of bills for September 14-28, 2021 - approved
Motion to schedule Beggar's Night on October 31, 2021 from 6-8 PM - approved
Motion to deny zone change ZC-2021-002 from R3 to M2 for 6823 Goshen Road - approved (denied the zone change)
Motion to accept $175 Kroger gift card from Becky Reed and Modern Woodsmen - approved
Motion to appoint Chris McMillan as full-time police officer effective October 10, 2021 - approved
action_items: Harry Holbert to meet with Jeff Kelly and neighbors to explore potential variance options for the truck repair business
Jeff Kelly encouraged to work with zoning director on a possible variance application
Autism Society training with fire department scheduled for October 7
Officer Rose administers body armor grant from Ohio Attorney General's Office
key_amounts: $6,366 body armor grant awarded from Ohio Attorney General's Office
$1,000 approximate cost per officer for body armor
$250,000 Jeff Kelly's stated investment in his business/building
$3,200 annual tax revenue from Jeff Kelly's parcel
$175 Kroger gift card donation from Becky Reed and Modern Woodsmen
100 sensory kits donated by Autism Society (50 for police, 50 for fire departments)
notable_topics: Autism Society of Greater Cincinnati sensory kit donation and first responder training
Public hearing on zoning case ZC-2021-002: zone change from R3 to M2 for truck repair business at 6823 Goshen Road
History of permit and address confusion between 6823 and 6825 Goshen Road
Allegations of prior zoning director issuing incorrect permits and providing misleading guidance
Hiring of new police officers to fill vacancies and meet 2020 levy staffing goals
Body armor grant awarded to police department
attendees: Mary Helen Rieker (Autism Society of Greater Cincinnati CEO)
notes: The zoning case was highly contentious with extensive public testimony both for and against the zone change. The applicant Jeff Kelly claimed he was misled by a former zoning director (Bob Seafrid) who issued permits for the wrong property and failed to advise him of the need for a zone change. The board took a recess at 8:53 PM to consult with the assistant prosecuting attorney before making their decision. The Clermont County Planning Commission and the Goshen Zoning Commission both recommended denial prior to the trustees' vote. The trustees encouraged the applicant to pursue a variance rather than a full zone change. The transcript was truncated during the police officer appointment discussion.
